2026-04-01T03:05:06.4267068Z 
2026-04-01T03:05:06.4267290Z   git switch -c <new-branch-name>

2026-04-01T03:05:07.3571213Z Repository permission level: admin
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3571841Z Redacted URL: chatgpt.com
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3572838Z Redacted URL: chatgpt.com
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3573975Z text: Support multiple model config slots and set Google AI Studio default to gemini-flash-latest
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3574725Z 
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3576618Z ### Motivation
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3577629Z - Change Google AI Studio default model from `gemini-2.5-pro` to `gemini-flash-latest` to match expected provider behavior.
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3579118Z - Fix the reload/save failure by making model settings loading more robust and returning server-persisted data after save.
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3581031Z - Prepare the UI and data model for multiple configuration slots so display names can change independently from a stable identifier.
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3581931Z 
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3582113Z ### Description
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3583859Z - Updated default model strings to `gemini-flash-latest` in the mobile app defaults and backend runtime fallback (`apps/mobile_chat_app/.../llm_config_service.dart`, `apps/mobile_chat_app/.../model_settings_screen.dart`, and `apps/node_backend/src/llm/llm_service.ts`).
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3586910Z - Refactored `LlmConfig` and `LlmConfigService` to add `slotId`, introduce `fetchConfigs()` (returns a list) while keeping `fetchDefault()` as a helper, and make `save()` return the server-persisted `LlmConfig` and persist `slot_id` in the JSON payload.
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3601123Z - Hardened API response parsing by safely converting dynamic maps and model preference structures when constructing `LlmConfig` instances.
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3604751Z - Reworked the model settings UI to show a top "Configs" selector row with `ChoiceChip` buttons whose labels are computed from each config's model name (falling back to `Config N`), maintain a stable ID hint, preserve the existing API-key blank-to-keep behavior, and keep state list-based to support future multiple slots.
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3607319Z 
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3607922Z ### Testing
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3608631Z - Ran `./tools/init_dev_env.sh` successfully to bootstrap the development environment.
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3610627Z - Ran `flutter analyze apps/mobile_chat_app/lib/features/settings/model_settings_screen.dart apps/mobile_chat_app/lib/features/settings/llm_config_service.dart` and it completed with no issues.
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3612455Z - Installed Node dependencies with `cd apps/node_backend && npm ci` and the command succeeded.
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3613701Z - Ran TypeScript type checking with `cd apps/node_backend && npm run type-check` and it completed successfully.
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3616024Z - Attempted `dart format` across Dart and TypeScript files which reported errors because a TypeScript file was passed to the Dart formatter, but Dart files were formatted; subsequent toolchain validation (`flutter analyze`, `npm ci`, `npm run type-check`) verified the changes.
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3617749Z 
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3617918Z ------
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3618324Z [Codex Task]((chatgpt.com/redacted)
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3619183Z title: Support multiple model config slots and set Google AI Studio default to gemini-flash-latest
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3620828Z body: ### Motivation
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3621796Z - Change Google AI Studio default model from `gemini-2.5-pro` to `gemini-flash-latest` to match expected provider behavior.
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3623280Z - Fix the reload/save failure by making model settings loading more robust and returning server-persisted data after save.
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3624814Z - Prepare the UI and data model for multiple configuration slots so display names can change independently from a stable identifier.
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3625693Z 
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3625867Z ### Description
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3633298Z - Updated default model strings to `gemini-flash-latest` in the mobile app defaults and backend runtime fallback (`apps/mobile_chat_app/.../llm_config_service.dart`, `apps/mobile_chat_app/.../model_settings_screen.dart`, and `apps/node_backend/src/llm/llm_service.ts`).
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3637008Z - Refactored `LlmConfig` and `LlmConfigService` to add `slotId`, introduce `fetchConfigs()` (returns a list) while keeping `fetchDefault()` as a helper, and make `save()` return the server-persisted `LlmConfig` and persist `slot_id` in the JSON payload.
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3639479Z - Hardened API response parsing by safely converting dynamic maps and model preference structures when constructing `LlmConfig` instances.
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3642856Z - Reworked the model settings UI to show a top "Configs" selector row with `ChoiceChip` buttons whose labels are computed from each config's model name (falling back to `Config N`), maintain a stable ID hint, preserve the existing API-key blank-to-keep behavior, and keep state list-based to support future multiple slots.
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3644812Z 
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3644992Z ### Testing
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3645752Z - Ran `./tools/init_dev_env.sh` successfully to bootstrap the development environment.
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3647911Z - Ran `flutter analyze apps/mobile_chat_app/lib/features/settings/model_settings_screen.dart apps/mobile_chat_app/lib/features/settings/llm_config_service.dart` and it completed with no issues.
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3651327Z - Installed Node dependencies with `cd apps/node_backend && npm ci` and the command succeeded.
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3653089Z - Ran TypeScript type checking with `cd apps/node_backend && npm run type-check` and it completed successfully.
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3655508Z - Attempted `dart format` across Dart and TypeScript files which reported errors because a TypeScript file was passed to the Dart formatter, but Dart files were formatted; subsequent toolchain validation (`flutter analyze`, `npm ci`, `npm run type-check`) verified the changes.
2026-04-01T03:05:07.3657257Z

// Call the substitution function
return await substitutePlaceholders({
  file: process.env.GH_AW_PROMPT,
  substitutions: {
    GH_AW_GITHUB_ACTOR: process.env.GH_AW_GITHUB_ACTOR,

2026-04-01T03:05:07.7666229Z 
2026-04-01T03:05:07.7666395Z ## Your Task
2026-04-01T03:05:07.7666614Z 
2026-04-01T03:05:07.7666845Z 1. Fetch the list of changed files in this PR.
2026-04-01T03:05:07.7667680Z 2. Read each changed file (focus on `.dart`, `.ts`, `.tsx`, `.js`, and migration SQL files).
2026-04-01T03:05:07.7668576Z 3. Apply the review criteria from the agent instructions.
2026-04-01T03:05:07.7669562Z 4. Post inline review comments only for real issues — do not comment on lines that are fine.
2026-04-01T03:05:07.7681173Z 
2026-04-01T03:05:07.7681405Z ## Review Priorities
2026-04-01T03:05:07.7681719Z 
2026-04-01T03:05:07.7682679Z 1. **Blockers** — security issues, null-safety violations, broken migrations, missing `mounted` guards, `undefined` passed as SQL params
2026-04-01T03:05:07.7684308Z 2. **Correctness** — logic errors, off-by-one errors, unhandled `async` errors
2026-04-01T03:05:07.7685568Z 3. **Convention** — ESM import style (`.js` extensions), Dart Effective Dart rules, missing dartdoc on public APIs
2026-04-01T03:05:07.7686654Z 4. **Tests** — new logic without corresponding tests
2026-04-01T03:05:07.7687078Z 
2026-04-01T03:05:07.7687255Z ## Output Format
2026-04-01T03:05:07.7687505Z 
2026-04-01T03:05:07.7688177Z For each issue found, create a **review comment** on the specific file and line using `create-pull-request-review-comment`.
2026-04-01T03:05:07.7689036Z 
2026-04-01T03:05:07.7689226Z Each comment should:
2026-04-01T03:05:07.7689700Z - Be concise (2–4 sentences max)
2026-04-01T03:05:07.7690489Z - State what the problem is
2026-04-01T03:05:07.7690973Z - State why it matters
2026-04-01T03:05:07.7691556Z - Suggest the fix
2026-04-01T03:05:07.7691822Z 
2026-04-01T03:05:07.7692068Z If no issues are found, do not post any comments.
